query: Memory as an Attack Surface in LLM Agents review

results.title: MindFort: Autonomous Security Engineer | Product Hunt
results.content:
AI Pentests — autonomous agentic assessments. Deploy a team of agents to hack your app, each with their own toolkit. Deploy agents in < 15 mins. First results in hours, not weeks.

AI Red Team — always-on adversarial agents that you can deploy on a schedule.

HillClimb — recursive learning. Agents consolidate memory and compound experience across attacks.

Validated Findings — MindFort gives you the exact steps to exploit and chain vulnerabilities.

Automated Remediation — every finding ships as a PR, with Github, Linear, and Jira integrations.

MF-1 — our custom offensive-security LLM. Built to chain exploits, break auth, and reason like an attacker

Defenders have to win every day. Attackers only need to win once.  
  
In the comments all day. AMA.  
— Brandon [...] MindFort

HillClimb is the part that hooked me , most pentest tools find the same surface-level issues every run because they don't learn. I'm curious how MindFort handles false positives at scale, since a PR for every finding gets noisy fast if validation isn't near-perfect.

Daniel Rabinovich
MindFort

MindFort

@sailikhith Each finding comes with steps to reproduce, and how the agent was able to exploit the vulnerability! Each agent also has context about your target, so it lowers false positives to almost none.

Eshita Kundu

#### What's great

Gemini is overall a great LLM for all the general tasks. Google AI Studio is very useful and Gemini is very easy to tune and use as a RAGged model in workflows.

#### What needs improvement

The model has a heavy reliance on short term memory and overall lacks the helpful assistance of following-up from chats that are further back. [...] Gemini

results.title: What are you building, and what does your stack look like? : General Discussion Forums | Product Hunt
results.content:
Report

Daniel Haven

Right now, it's a simple tracker. I use it as a way to motivate myself to get out of bed on time. The fewer minutes I spend in bed, the nicer my stats look on the home screen.

Also, I expose Apple Shortcuts actions for reading, creating, and updating logs.

Report

Pashupathi Mali

I’m building RoutePlex, a vendor-neutral AI gateway & model router for teams running multiple LLM providers in production.

We’re focused on solving the operational layer of AI: routing, fallback logic, quota enforcement, and cost predictability across providers.

Beyond basic routing, RoutePlex includes a prompt enhancement layer and a lightweight learning system that adapts routing decisions based on performance patterns. [...] FWIW the most interesting part isn't the stack, it's that the agent is effectively my cofounder. it reads my messages, browses the web, posts comments (including this one tbh), manages memory across sessions. the "stack" conversation matters way less when your agent can swap out any component in an afternoon

Report

Ryan Hendrickson

@umairnadeem Interesting that you're leaning into the 'slop' title in your company name -- are you worried about that invoking negative connotations with your audience? Very interesting as well that your effective cofounder is an OpenClaw instance. Is it deploying subinstances to work on the code, or is it directly doing the work?

Report

Umair [...] Report

Pashupathi Mali

@ryan\_hendrickson   
Yes, teams integrate once with RoutePlex and get access to multiple providers through a unified API surface.

We support strategy-based routing (cost caps, provider priority, quotas) along with automatic failover and cost tracking.

In addition, routing decisions can adapt over time based on observed performance patterns - such as latency, reliability, and cost efficiency , so it’s not just static fallback logic

Report

Umair

building openslop.ai - free open-source AI video creation workflows. solo founder, building the whole thing in public with my AI agent (OpenClaw running Claude Opus 4.6). the agent literally handles everything from writing code to posting on social media to managing my calendar.
